title: Rosa vaillantiana
intro: Rosa vaillantiana, also known as Vaillant's rose or mountain rose, is a species of rose native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rosa vaillantiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Rosa vaillantiana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Rosa vaillantiana, also known as the Vaillant's rose, is a species of rose that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family or the rose order. The family Rosaceae includes over 4,000 species, many of which are deciduous or evergreen shrubs and trees. The genus Rosa includes about 150 species, many of which are popular ornamental plants. Rosa vaillantiana is native to China and is a member of the genus Rosa.
